tests.source_program:
declare double @pow(double, double)
declare double @llvm.fabs.f64(double)

define double @pow_libcall_half_switch_domination(double %x) {
entry:
  %a = call double @llvm.fabs.f64(double %x)
  %c = fcmp oeq double %a, 0x7FF0000000000000
  %switch_val = zext i1 %c to i32
  switch i32 %switch_val, label %compute [
    i32 1, label %inf_case
  ]

inf_case:
  ret double 0.000000e+00

compute:
  %retval = call double @pow(double %x, double 5.000000e-01)
  ret double %retval
}

  %switch_val = zext i1 %c to i32

  ]

inf_case:

compute:

  ret double %retval
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary specifically mentions 'fcmp oeq with llvm.fabs in a dominating block' with branch instructions, but this uses switch instruction for control flow, requiring DomConditionCache to track conditions through switch edges.

tests.source_program:
declare double @pow(double, double)
declare double @llvm.fabs.f64(double)

define double @pow_libcall_half_phi_incoming_check(double %x, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %true_branch, label %false_branch

true_branch:
  %a1 = call double @llvm.fabs.f64(double %x)
  %c1 = fcmp oeq double %a1, 0x7FF0000000000000
  br i1 %c1, label %inf_exit, label %merge

false_branch:
  %a2 = call double @llvm.fabs.f64(double %x)
  %c2 = fcmp oeq double %a2, 0x7FF0000000000000
  br i1 %c2, label %inf_exit, label %merge

inf_exit:
  ret double 0.000000e+00

merge:
  %x.phi = phi double [ %x, %true_branch ], [ %x, %false_branch ]
  %retval = call double @pow(double %x.phi, double 5.000000e-01)
  ret double %retval
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare double @pow(double, double)
declare double @llvm.fabs.f64(double)

define double @pow_libcall_half_phi_incoming_check(double %x, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %true_branch, label %false_branch

true_branch:
  %a1 = call double @llvm.fabs.f64(double %x)
  %c1 = fcmp oeq double %a1, 0x7FF0000000000000
  br i1 %c1, label %inf_exit, label %merge

false_branch:
  %a2 = call double @llvm.fabs.f64(double %x)
  %c2 = fcmp oeq double %a2, 0x7FF0000000000000
  br i1 %c2, label %inf_exit, label %merge

inf_exit:
  ret double 0.000000e+00

merge:
  %x.phi = phi double [ %x, %true_branch ], [ %x, %false_branch ]
  %retval = call double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double %x.phi)
  ret double %retval
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes direct dominance from a single block, but this test has the infinity check split across two predecessor blocks with the value coming through a phi node, requiring DomConditionCache to merge condition information from multiple dominating paths.

  %c1 = fcmp oeq double %a1, 0x7FF0000000000000

false_branch:
  %a2 = call double @llvm.fabs.f64(double %x)
  %c2 = fcmp oeq double %a2, 0x7FF0000000000000
  br i1 %c2, label %inf_exit, label %merge

inf_exit:

  %x.phi = phi double [ %x, %true_branch ], [ %x, %false_branch ]
  %retval = call double @pow(double %x.phi, double 5.000000e-01)

}

tests.source_program:
declare double @pow(double, double)
declare double @llvm.fabs.f64(double)

define double @pow_libcall_half_phi_dominance(double %x) {
entry:
  %a = call double @llvm.fabs.f64(double %x)
  %c = fcmp oeq double %a, 0x7FF0000000000000
  br i1 %c, label %inf_block, label %merge

inf_block:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi = phi double [ %x, %entry ], [ %x, %inf_block ]
  %retval = call double @pow(double %phi, double 5.000000e-01)
  ret double %retval
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double)
declare double @llvm.fabs.f64(double)

define double @pow_libcall_half_phi_dominance(double %x) {
entry:
  %a = call double @llvm.fabs.f64(double %x)
  %c = fcmp oeq double %a, 0x7FF0000000000000
  br i1 %c, label %inf_block, label %merge

inf_block:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi = phi double [ %x, %entry ], [ %x, %inf_block ]
  %retval = call double @llvm.sqrt.f64(double %phi)
  ret double %retval
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes direct fcmp+fabs in dominating block, but this test uses a phi node where dominance proves all incoming values are safe. The DomConditionCache enables tracking through phi nodes, which is beyond the specific pattern described.
